brand.name: Acko Health Insurance
brand.tagline: Save 18% instantly with ZERO GST health plans

offer.what: Direct-to-consumer health insurance (Standard and Platinum plans) with cashless treatment at 11,500+ hospitals, sold entirely online without agents or paperwork
offer.coreOutcome: Complete peace of mind that a medical emergency won't devastate your finances — 100% of hospital bills covered, zero co-pay, zero waiting period
offer.pricePoint: Starting at ₹18/day (~₹540/month); annual subscription; sum insured from ₹10 lakh to unlimited
offer.commitment: Annual renewable subscription; no lock-in beyond the policy year
leadMagnets: Free medical check-up for eligible applicants
Instant digital policy issuance (no paperwork, no agent)

audience.icp: Urban Indian professional, age 25–45, tech-savvy, currently underinsured or frustrated with traditional insurer bureaucracy and opaque claims processes
painPoints: Fear that claims will be denied or only partially paid at hospital discharge
Hidden co-pays and room rent sub-limits that erode real coverage
Long disease-specific waiting periods making new policies feel useless
Agent middlemen adding cost, confusion, and commission
Paperwork-heavy reimbursement processes during a medical crisis
segments: Young families with children (family floater plans)
Mid-career adults (age 35–50) upgrading after a health scare or life event
Senior citizens (60+) seeking cashless hospital coverage
NRIs needing India-based health cover for themselves or parents

salesCycle.length: Days to 2–3 weeks (considered purchase, but accelerated by fully digital self-serve flow)
salesCycle.trial: No free trial; premium calculator provides an instant no-commitment quote; free medical check-up offered to eligible applicants post-application
salesCycle.closeMechanism: Self-serve digital signup; instant online policy issuance; no salesperson or agent in the loop
salesCycle.topObjection: Can I trust a newer insurer to actually pay my claim? Will the 98% settlement rate hold when it is my claim?

ahaMoment: Receiving an instant digital policy document minutes after payment — and later, having a cashless claim approved within hours at a network hospital with zero paperwork

retention.recurring: Annual premium renewal; policy lapses if not renewed on time
retention.expansion: Upgrade Standard to Platinum plan; add family members to floater; increase sum insured; add top-up or OPD riders
retention.churnRisk: Annual renewal window — customers with no claims may question value; competitors actively target Acko policyholders at renewal with cheaper base quotes

lifecycleGaps: Post-purchase onboarding: customers likely don't know how to use the cashless network, initiate pre-authorisation, or file a claim before they need to
Mid-year value reinforcement: little or no touchpoint between policy issuance and renewal reminder
Pre-renewal nurture: renewal outreach likely price-only with no claims story or value narrative
Lead-magnet nurture: quote-calculator visitors who don't convert likely receive no structured follow-up sequence
Referral and advocacy: 8 crore customers are an underutilised referral engine
Win-back: lapsed policyholders (non-renewals) likely not systematically re-engaged with updated offers
toneVoice: Direct, empowering, anti-jargon; conversational but confident; 'people before paperwork' ethos; no fear-mongering — leads with simplicity, zero-hassle, and transparency rather than catastrophe scenarios
compliance: IRDAI regulated (License #157); all claims statistics and product statements must comply with IRDAI advertising guidelines; India DPDP Act governs data privacy; TRAI DLT registration required for commercial email/SMS; avoid absolute claim guarantees beyond stated settlement statistics; no medical advice
